title: Ipomoea malvacea
intro: Ipomoea malvacea is a type of plant commonly known as the moonflower or white moonflower, belonging to the Convolvulaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, preventing infections, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea malvacea include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Ipomoea malvacea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. This plant is part of the genus Ipomoea, which contains around 600 to 700 species of morning glories. The Convolvulaceae family is a large group of plants, often characterized by twining stems and trumpet-shaped flowers. Ipomoea malvacea is native to tropical regions and is often found in areas with warm and humid climates. The classification of Ipomoea malvacea reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Convolvulaceae family.
